content_of_post_to_comment: People do not realize how strong they are until being strong is the only option they have. Nobody wakes up one morning and says, I hope life gives me more than I can handle. But life does. And somehow, people survive things they once thought would destroy them. Not because they wanted to be strong. Not because they were fearless. But because there were bills to pay. Children to raise. Appointments to keep. And mornings that still came. Sometimes strength is not courage. Sometimes it is simply having no other choice. And yet, you keep going. That, too, is strength.
comment: One thing I wonder about: when strength comes from having no alternative, people often don't recognize it as strength until much later. In the moment it can feel more like inertia than resilience.
